usage_notes: Used for nuclear and extended family alike.
common_collocations: eine Familie haben, eine große Familie, meine Familie
image_context: A happy family of four sitting together at home
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable: fa-MI-lie. Final -e is a schwa /ə/.
related_words: der Vater, die Mutter, das Kind, der Bruder, die Schwester

usage_notes: Formal/full form; 'Papa' is the informal equivalent.
common_collocations: mein Vater, der Vater von, Vater sein
image_context: A father holding hands with his child in a park
example_3: Ich gehe mit meinem Vater spazieren.
example_3_translation: Eu vou passear com meu pai.
pronunciation_tips: The 'V' is pronounced as /f/. The final -er is a reduced /ɐ/.
memory_hook: Sounds like 'father' with a German accent.
related_words: die Mutter, das Kind, die Familie, der Sohn, die Tochter

usage_notes: Formal form; 'Mama' is the informal equivalent.
common_collocations: meine Mutter, die Mutter von, Mutter werden
image_context: A mother hugging her child at home
example_2: Die Mutter liest dem Kind vor.
example_2_translation: A mãe lê para a criança.
pronunciation_tips: Short /ʊ/ vowel. The umlaut plural Mütter has /ʏ/.
memory_hook: Sounds like 'mother' — very similar across Germanic languages.
related_words: der Vater, das Kind, die Familie, die Tochter, der Sohn

common_collocations: mein Bruder, ein älterer Bruder, ein jüngerer Bruder
image_context: Two brothers playing together outside
example_2: Mein Bruder ist zwölf Jahre alt.
example_3: Mein Bruder und ich spielen Fußball.
example_3_translation: Meu irmão e eu jogamos futebol.
pronunciation_tips: The 'r' in 'Bruder' is uvular /ʁ/. Final -er is /ɐ/.
related_words: die Schwester, die Geschwister, der Vater, die Mutter

usage_notes: In hospital contexts 'Schwester' means nurse.
common_collocations: meine Schwester, eine ältere Schwester, eine jüngere Schwester
image_context: Two sisters smiling and standing together
pronunciation_tips: 'Sch' = /ʃ/. 'W' in 'Schw-' = /v/.
memory_hook: Think of 'sister' — Schwester shares the '-ster' ending.
related_words: der Bruder, die Geschwister, die Mutter, der Vater

usage_notes: Neutral; does not specify gender. Plural 'Kinder' also means children.
common_collocations: ein Kind haben, ein kleines Kind, das Kind spielt
image_context: A young child playing happily in a park
pronunciation_tips: Final 'd' is devoiced to /t/ in German.
related_words: die Eltern, der Vater, die Mutter, der Sohn, die Tochter

usage_notes: Always used in the plural. There is no singular form in common use.
common_collocations: meine Eltern, die Eltern von, bei den Eltern wohnen
image_context: Two parents with their children at home
example_3: Meine Eltern arbeiten in der Stadt.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable. Final -n is clearly pronounced.
memory_hook: Contains 'elt-' related to 'alt' (old) — the older generation.
related_words: der Vater, die Mutter, das Kind, die Familie
common_mistakes: Treating 'Eltern' as singular or using a singular article.

common_collocations: mein Sohn, ein kleiner Sohn, der älteste Sohn
image_context: A father walking with his young son
example_1: Mein Sohn ist sechs Jahre alt.
pronunciation_tips: 'S' at start of word before vowel = /z/. Long /oː/ vowel.
related_words: die Tochter, das Kind, der Vater, die Mutter

common_collocations: meine Tochter, die älteste Tochter, eine kleine Tochter
image_context: A mother and daughter smiling together at home
example_3: Er hat eine Tochter und einen Sohn.
example_3_translation: Ele tem uma filha e um filho.
pronunciation_tips: 'ch' after /ɔ/ is the guttural /x/ as in 'Bach'.
memory_hook: Cognate with English 'daughter' — both from Proto-Germanic.
related_words: der Sohn, das Kind, die Mutter, der Vater

usage_notes: Informal equivalent: 'Opa'.
common_collocations: mein Großvater, der Großvater von, beim Großvater
image_context: An elderly grandfather sitting with his grandchild on a bench
example_3: Ich besuche meinen Großvater am Wochenende.
example_3_translation: Eu visito meu avô no fim de semana.
pronunciation_tips: Compound: 'groß' (/ɡʁoːs/) + 'Vater' (/faːtɐ/). 'V' = /f/.
related_words: die Großmutter, der Opa, der Enkel, die Familie

usage_notes: Informal equivalent: 'Oma'.
common_collocations: meine Großmutter, bei der Großmutter, die Großmutter besuchen
image_context: A grandmother baking with her grandchild in the kitchen
pronunciation_tips: Compound: 'groß' + 'Mutter'. Short /ʊ/ in 'Mutter'.
related_words: der Großvater, die Oma, die Enkelin, die Familie

usage_notes: Plural form is identical to the singular.
common_collocations: mein Onkel, der Onkel von, beim Onkel
image_search_query: uncle nephew niece playing together family
image_context: An uncle playing with his niece and nephew
example_3_translation: Eu gosto muito do meu tio.
pronunciation_tips: The 'nk' cluster is /ŋk/. Final -el is syllabic /l̩/.
memory_hook: Very similar to English 'uncle' and Portuguese 'tio' — think 'Onkel = Uncle'.
related_words: die Tante, der Cousin, die Cousine, die Familie

common_collocations: meine Tante, die Tante von, bei der Tante
image_context: An aunt smiling with her niece at a family gathering
example_1_translation: Minha tia faz um bolo delicioso.
example_2: Die Tante kommt heute zu Besuch.
example_3: Ich telefoniere oft mit meiner Tante.
example_3_translation: Eu telefono frequentemente para minha tia.
pronunciation_tips: Short /a/ in first syllable. Final -e is schwa /ə/.
memory_hook: Sounds like English 'aunt' with a 't' ending.
related_words: der Onkel, die Cousine, der Cousin, die Familie

usage_notes: Borrowed from French; the female form is 'Cousine'.
common_collocations: mein Cousin, der Cousin von, mit dem Cousin spielen
image_context: Two male cousins playing together at a family event
example_3: Ich spiele mit meinem Cousin Fußball.
example_3_translation: Eu jogo futebol com meu primo.
pronunciation_tips: French loanword — final nasal vowel /ɛ̃/ may be simplified to /ɛn/ in casual speech.
related_words: die Cousine, der Onkel, die Tante, die Familie

usage_notes: Female counterpart of 'Cousin'. Also spelled 'Kusine' in some regions.
common_collocations: meine Cousine, die Cousine von, mit der Cousine
image_context: Two female cousins smiling together at a family gathering
example_3: Ich schreibe meiner Cousine eine Nachricht.
example_3_translation: Eu escrevo uma mensagem para minha prima.
pronunciation_tips: Long /iː/ in second syllable. Final -e is schwa /ə/.
memory_hook: Think 'cousin' + feminine -e ending, like French/Romance pattern.
related_words: der Cousin, der Onkel, die Tante, die Familie

usage_notes: Context determines meaning: 'mein Mann' = my husband; 'ein Mann' = a man.
common_collocations: mein Mann, ein junger Mann, der Mann auf der Straße
image_context: An adult man standing and smiling
pronunciation_tips: Short /a/. Double 'nn' confirms the short vowel.
related_words: die Frau, der Ehemann, die Familie, der Vater

usage_notes: Meine Frau = my wife; also used as the honorific 'Frau Schmidt'.
common_collocations: meine Frau, eine junge Frau, Frau Müller
image_context: An adult woman smiling warmly
pronunciation_tips: Diphthong /aʊ̯/ — glides from /a/ to /ʊ/. 'Fr-' = /fʁ/.
memory_hook: Think of 'Frau' as the female counterpart of 'Mann'.
related_words: der Mann, die Ehefrau, die Mutter, die Familie

usage_notes: English loanword fully integrated into German. Takes 'das'.
common_collocations: ein Baby bekommen, das Baby schläft, ein Baby stillen
image_context: A newborn baby sleeping peacefully in a crib
pronunciation_tips: Long /eː/ in first syllable. Ending pronounced /bi/, not /bi:/.
related_words: das Kind, die Mutter, der Vater, die Familie

usage_notes: Refers specifically to a male grandchild. Female: 'die Enkelin'.
common_collocations: mein Enkel, der Enkel von, den Enkel besuchen
image_context: A grandfather sitting with his grandson on a park bench
pronunciation_tips: The 'nk' is /ŋk/. Final -el is syllabic /l̩/.
related_words: die Enkelin, der Großvater, die Großmutter, die Familie

usage_notes: Female form of 'Enkel'. Plural adds '-innen'.
common_collocations: meine Enkelin, die Enkelin von, die Enkelin besuchen
image_context: A grandmother hugging her granddaughter lovingly
example_2_translation: A neta está pintando um quadro.
example_3: Die Großmutter passt auf ihre Enkelin auf.
example_3_translation: A avó cuida de sua neta.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: ÉN-ke-lin. Stress on first syllable.
related_words: der Enkel, die Großmutter, der Großvater, die Familie

usage_notes: Typically plural; the singular refers to one sibling (less common).
common_collocations: meine Geschwister, Geschwister haben, keine Geschwister
image_context: A group of siblings laughing together
pronunciation_tips: Prefix 'Ge-' is unstressed /ɡə/. 'Schw' = /ʃv/.
related_words: der Bruder, die Schwester, die Familie, die Eltern
common_mistakes: Treating it as masculine ('der') instead of 'die'.

usage_notes: Adjectival noun: 'der/die Verwandte'. Inflects like an adjective.
common_collocations: meine Verwandten, entfernte Verwandte, Verwandte besuchen
image_context: A large group of relatives gathered for a family celebration
example_1_translation: Meus parentes vêm para a festa.
example_2: Er hat viele Verwandte in Bayern.
example_2_translation: Ele tem muitos parentes na Baviera.
pronunciation_tips: Prefix 'ver-' = /fɛɐ̯/. Stress on second syllable.
memory_hook: From 'verwandt' (related/related to) → those who are related to you.
related_words: die Familie, der Onkel, die Tante, der Cousin
common_mistakes: Not inflecting the adjectival noun correctly in different cases.

usage_notes: 'Mein Freund' can mean 'my (male) friend' or 'my boyfriend' depending on context.
common_collocations: ein guter Freund, mein bester Freund, Freunde treffen
image_context: Two male friends laughing and hanging out together
pronunciation_tips: Diphthong /ɔʏ̯/. Final 'd' devoiced to /t/.
memory_hook: Root of 'Freundschaft' (friendship) — recognizable in many contexts.
related_words: die Freundin, die Freundschaft, der Bekannte, die Familie
common_mistakes: Using 'die' instead of 'der'; confusing 'friend' vs 'boyfriend' context.

usage_notes: 'Meine Freundin' can mean 'my (female) friend' or 'my girlfriend' depending on context.
common_collocations: meine Freundin, eine gute Freundin, die Freundin treffen
image_context: Two female friends smiling together at a café
example_2: Ich gehe mit meiner Freundin ins Kino.
example_2_translation: Eu vou ao cinema com minha amiga.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: FRÖY-ndin. Diphthong /ɔʏ̯/ as in 'Freund'.
related_words: der Freund, die Freundschaft, die Bekannte, die Familie

usage_notes: Female form: 'die Nachbarin'. Plural 'Nachbarn' covers both genders.
common_collocations: mein Nachbar, ein guter Nachbar, den Nachbarn grüßen
image_context: A man greeting his neighbour at the door
pronunciation_tips: 'ch' after /a/ = /x/. Final -r is vocalised to /ɐ̯/.
memory_hook: 'Nach' = near + 'bar' (related to 'gebären' = to bear/carry) → one who lives nearby.
related_words: die Nachbarin, die Straße, das Haus, die Gemeinde
